amendments.version_after_text: Each house shall keep a journal of its proceedings, and a recorded vote on any question shall be taken at the request of one-sixth of those members present. No bill may become law except by a recorded vote of a majority of the members elected to each house, and the lieutenant governor is considered a member-elect of the senate when the lieutenant governor votes. No law may be enacted except by a bill passed by both houses, and no bill may be amended on its passage through either house in a manner which changes its general subject matter. No bill may embrace more than one subject, which must be expressed in its title; but a law violating this provision is invalid only to the extent the subject is not so expressed. Every bill must be read on two separate natural days, and the readings may be by title only unless a reading at length is demanded by one-fifth of the members present. No bill may be amended, extended, or incorporated in any other bill by reference to its title only, except in the case of definitions and procedural provisions. The presiding officer of each house shall sign all bills passed and resolutions adopted by the legislative assembly, and the fact of signing shall be entered at once in the journal. Every law enacted by the legislative assembly takes effect on July first after its filing with the secretary of state or on ninety days after its filing whichever comes later, or on a subsequent date if specified in the law unless, by a vote of two-thirds of the members elected to each house, the legislative assembly declares it an emergency measure and includes the declaration in the Act. An emergency measure takes effect upon its filing with the secretary of state or on a date specified in the measure. Every law enacted by a special session of the legislative assembly takes effect on a date specified in the Act.
amendments.notes: 1985 S.L. ch. 707 (Measure 2, gen. election Nov. 6, 1984), SECTION 2: 'A new section to article IV ... is hereby created and enacted.' Fully new text, nothing struck. This is the chain HEAD for art. IV s 13. Verbatim from CAA.pdf p.8 (re-rendered 400 dpi). Effective-date clause then read 'Every law enacted by the legislative assembly takes effect on July first after its filing with the secretary of state or on ninety days after its filing whichever comes later'.

amendments.version_after_text: Each house shall keep a journal of its proceedings, and a recorded vote on any question shall be taken at the request of one-sixth of those members present. No bill may become law except by a recorded vote of a majority of the members elected to each house, and the lieutenant governor is considered a member-elect of the senate when the lieutenant governor votes. No law may be enacted except by a bill passed by both houses, and no bill may be amended on its passage through either house in a manner which changes its general subject matter. No bill may embrace more than one subject, which must be expressed in its title; but a law violating this provision is invalid only to the extent the subject is not so expressed. Every bill must be read on two separate natural days, and the readings may be by title only unless a reading at length is demanded by one-fifth of the members present. No bill may be amended, extended, or incorporated in any other bill by reference to its title only, except in the case of definitions and procedural provisions. The presiding officer of each house shall sign all bills passed and resolutions adopted by the legislative assembly, and the fact of signing shall be entered at once in the journal. Every law, except as otherwise provided in this section, enacted by the legislative assembly takes effect on July first after its filing with the secretary of state or on ninety days after its filing whichever comes later, or on a subsequent date if specified in the law unless, by a vote of two-thirds of the members elected to each house, the legislative assembly declares it an emergency measure and includes the declaration in the Act. Every appropriation measure for support and maintenance of state departments and institutions and every tax measure that changes tax rates enacted by the legislative assembly take effect on July first after its filing with the secretary of state or on a subsequent date if specified in the law unless, by a vote of two-thirds of the members elected to each house, the legislative assembly declares it an emergency measure and includes the declaration in the Act. An emergency measure takes effect upon its filing with the secretary of state or on a date specified in the measure. Every law enacted by a special session of the legislative assembly takes effect on a date specified in the Act. The legislative assembly shall enact all laws necessary to carry into effect the provisions of this constitution. Except as otherwise provided in this constitution, no local or special laws may be enacted, nor may the legislative assembly indirectly enact special or local laws by the partial repeal of a general law but laws repealing local or special laws may be enacted.

amendments.notes: 1987 S.L. ch. 778 (Measure 1, special election), SECTION 1, amending art. IV s 13. Purely additive (no strikeouts). Added: (1) 'except as otherwise provided in this section,' after 'Every law'; (2) the new sentence 'Every appropriation measure for support and maintenance of state departments and institutions and every tax measure that changes tax rates enacted by the legislative assembly take effect on July first after its filing with the secretary of state or on a subsequent date if specified in the law unless, by a vote of two-thirds of the members elected to each house, the legislative assembly declares it an emergency measure and includes the declaration in the Act.'; (3) the final paragraph 'The legislative assembly shall enact all laws necessary to carry into effect the provisions of this constitution. Except as otherwise provided in this constitution, no local or special laws may be enacted...'. Verbatim from 1987 CAA.pdf pp.10-11 (450 dpi).

amendments.version_after_text: Each house shall keep a journal of its proceedings, and a recorded vote on any question shall be taken at the request of one-sixth of those members present. No bill may become law except by a recorded vote of a majority of the members elected to each house, and the lieutenant governor is considered a member-elect of the senate when the lieutenant governor votes. No law may be enacted except by a bill passed by both houses, and no bill may be amended on its passage through either house in a manner which changes its general subject matter. No bill may embrace more than one subject, which must be expressed in its title; but a law violating this provision is invalid only to the extent the subject is not so expressed. Every bill must be read on two separate natural days, and the readings may be by title only unless a reading at length is demanded by one-fifth of the members present. No bill may be amended, extended, or incorporated in any other bill by reference to its title only, except in the case of definitions and procedural provisions. The presiding officer of each house shall sign all bills passed and resolutions adopted by the legislative assembly, and the fact of signing shall be entered at once in the journal. Every law, except as otherwise provided in this section, enacted by the legislative assembly during its eighty natural meeting days takes effect on August first after its filing with the secretary of state, or if filed on or after August first and before January first of the following year ninety days after its filing, or on a subsequent date if specified in the law unless, by a vote of two-thirds of the members elected to each house, the legislative assembly declares it an emergency measure and includes the declaration in the Act. Every appropriation measure for support and maintenance of state departments and institutions and every tax measure that changes tax rates enacted by the legislative assembly take effect on July first after its filing with the secretary of state or on a subsequent date if specified in the law unless, by a vote of two-thirds of the members elected to each house, the legislative assembly declares it an emergency measure and includes the declaration in the Act. An emergency measure takes effect upon its filing with the secretary of state or on a date specified in the measure. Every law enacted by a special session of the legislative assembly takes effect on a date specified in the Act. The legislative assembly shall enact all laws necessary to carry into effect the provisions of this constitution. Except as otherwise provided in this constitution, no local or special laws may be enacted, nor may the legislative assembly indirectly enact special or local laws by the partial repeal of a general law but laws repealing local or special laws may be enacted.

amendments.notes: 1991 S.L. ch. 659 (HCR 3016; Measure 1, gen. election Nov. 3, 1992), SECTION 1, amending art. IV s 13. In the 'Every law' effective-date sentence: ADDED 'during its eighty natural meeting days' (after 'legislative assembly'); changed 'July' to 'August'; ADDED comma after 'secretary of state'; ADDED 'if filed on or after August first and before January first of the following year'; STRUCK 'whichever comes later'. version_after equals current_text. Verbatim from 1993 CAA.pdf pp.2-3 (450 dpi).

amendments.version_after_text: While serving in the legislative assembly, no member may hold any full-time appointive state office established by this constitution or designated by law. During the term for which elected, no member of the legislative assembly may be appointed to any full-time office which has been created, or to any office for which the compensation has been increased, by the legislative assembly during that term.
amendments.notes: 1985 S.L. ch. 706 (HCR 3028, ch. 728; Measure on 1984 gen. election), SECTION 6: 'A new section to article IV ... is hereby created and enacted.' Chain HEAD. The chapter created arts. IV ss 1-18 as a unit; this SECTION 6 is constitutional art. IV s 6 (matches the pre-amendment text shown struck/restored in the 2012 redline). Created text: '...appointed to any full-time office which has been created, or to any office for which the compensation has been increased, by the legislative assembly during that term.' Verbatim from 1985 CAA.pdf p.5 (450 dpi).

amendments.version_after_text: While serving in the legislative assembly, no member may hold any full-time appointive state office established by this constitution or designated by law. During the term for which elected, no member of the legislative assembly may be appointed to any full-time office that has been created by the legislative assembly. During the term for which elected, no member of the legislative assembly may be appointed to any full-time office for which the legislative assembly has increased the compensation in an amount greater than the general rate of increase provided to full-time state employees.
amendments.version_before_text: While serving in the legislative assembly, no member may hold any full-time appointive state office established by this constitution or designated by law. During the term for which elected, no member of the legislative assembly may be appointed to any full-time office which has been created, or to any office for which the compensation has been increased, by the legislative assembly during that term.
reverse_edit_list: to any full-time office that has been created by the legislative assembly. During the term for which elected, no member of the legislative assembly may be appointed to any full-time office for which the legislative assembly has increased the compensation in an amount greater than the general rate of increase provided to full-time state employees.
to any full-time office which has been created, or to any office for which the compensation has been increased, by the legislative assembly during that term.
amendments.notes: 2012 S.L. (63rd assembly) ch. 515 (HCR 3047; Measure 1, 2012 primary), SECTION 1, amending art. IV s 6. Restructured the last two sentences. Changes (after->before): 'office that has been created by the legislative assembly. During the term for which elected, no member of the legislative assembly may be appointed to any full-time office for which the legislative assembly has increased the compensation in an amount greater than the general rate of increase provided to full-time state employees.' replaced 'office which has been created, or to any office for which the compensation has been increased, by the legislative assembly during that term.' i.e. 'which'->'that'; struck ', or'; added 'by the legislative assembly. During the term for which elected, no member of the legislative assembly may be appointed' and 'full-time'; struck 'compensation has been increased, by the' and 'during that term'; added 'has increased the compensation in an amount greater than the general rate of increase provided to full-time state employees'. version_after equals current_text. Verbatim from 2013 caa.pdf p.1 (450 dpi). Single contiguous reverse edit used to keep after_phrase unique.

amendments.version_after_text: Taxes shall be uniform upon the same class of property including franchises within the territorial limits of the authority levying the tax. The legislative assembly may by law exempt any or all classes of personal property from taxation and within the meaning of this section, fixtures, buildings and improvements of every character, whatsoever, upon land shall be deemed personal property. The property of the United States, to the extent immunity from taxation has not been waived by an act of Congress, property of the state, county, and municipal corporations, to the extent immunity from taxation has not been waived by an act of the legislative assembly, and property used exclusively for schools, religious, cemetery, charitable or other public purposes shall be exempt from taxation. Except as restricted by this article, the legislative assembly may provide for raising revenue and fixing the situs of all property for the purpose of taxation. Provided that all taxes and exemptions in force when this amendment is adopted shall remain in force until otherwise provided by statute.
amendments.version_before_text: Taxes shall be uniform upon the same class of property including franchises within the territorial limits of the authority levying the tax. The legislature may by law exempt any or all classes of personal property from taxation and within the meaning of this section, fixtures, buildings and improvements of every character, whatsoever, upon land shall be deemed personal property. The property of the United States and property of the state, county, and municipal corporations, and property used exclusively for schools, religious, cemetery, charitable or other public purposes shall be exempt from taxation. Except as restricted by this article, the legislature may provide for raising revenue and fixing the situs of all property for the purpose of taxation. Provided that all taxes and exemptions in force when this amendment is adopted shall remain in force until otherwise provided by statute.
reverse_edit_list: The property of the United States, to the extent immunity from taxation has not been waived by an act of Congress, property of the state, county, and municipal corporations, to the extent immunity from taxation has not been waived by an act of the legislative assembly, and property used exclusively
The property of the United States and property of the state, county, and municipal corporations, and property used exclusively
The legislative assembly may by law exempt any or all classes
The legislature may by law exempt any or all classes
Except as restricted by this article, the legislative assembly may provide
Except as restricted by this article, the legislature may provide
amendments.notes: 1989 S.L. ch. 788 ('Federal Land Bank Taxation'; SCR 4047, ch. 755, 1987 S.L.; Measure on 1988 gen. election), SECTION 1, amending art. X s 5. EARLIEST modern amendment; not a create (art. X s 5 carried from the 1981 reorganization), so version_before is the carried-from-1981 head. Changes: 'legislature'->'legislative assembly' (twice: 'The legislature may by law exempt...' and 'Except as restricted by this article, the legislature may provide...'); in the U.S.-property clause struck 'and' after 'United States' and added the two immunity clauses 'to the extent immunity from taxation has not been waived by an act of Congress' and 'to the extent immunity from taxation has not been waived by an act of the legislative assembly,'. Verbatim from 1989 CAA.pdf pp.1-2 (450 dpi). U.S.-property clause reversed as one contiguous edit for uniqueness.

amendments.version_after_text: Taxes shall be uniform upon the same class of property including franchises within the territorial limits of the authority levying the tax. The legislative assembly may by law exempt any or all classes of personal property from taxation and within the meaning of this section, fixtures, buildings and improvements of every character, whatsoever, upon land shall be deemed personal property. The property of the United States, to the extent immunity from taxation has not been waived by an act of Congress, property of the state, county, and municipal corporations, to the extent immunity from taxation has not been waived by an act of the legislative assembly, and property used exclusively for schools, religious, cemetery, charitable or other public purposes shall be exempt from taxation. Real property used for conservation or wildlife purposes is not exempt from taxation unless an exemption is provided by the legislative assembly. Except as restricted by this article, the legislative assembly may provide for raising revenue and fixing the situs of all property for the purpose of taxation. Provided that all taxes and exemptions in force when this amendment is adopted shall remain in force until otherwise provided by statute.
amendments.version_before_text: Taxes shall be uniform upon the same class of property including franchises within the territorial limits of the authority levying the tax. The property of the United States, to the extent immunity from taxation has not been waived by an act of Congress, property of the state, county, and municipal corporations, to the extent immunity from taxation has not been waived by an act of the legislative assembly, and property used exclusively for schools, religious, cemetery, charitable or other public purposes shall be exempt from taxation. Except as restricted by this article, the legislative assembly may provide for raising revenue and fixing the situs of all property for the purpose of taxation. Provided that all taxes and exemptions in force when this amendment is adopted shall remain in force until otherwise provided by statute.
reverse_edit_list: Real property used for conservation or wildlife purposes is not exempt from taxation unless an exemption is provided by the legislative assembly.
amendments.notes: 2003 S.L. (58th assembly) ch. 577 ('Taxable Status of Land Held for Conservation Purposes'; SCR 4045; Measure on 2002 gen. election), SECTION 1, amending art. X s 5. Purely additive: added the sentence 'Real property used for conservation or wildlife purposes is not exempt from taxation unless an exemption is provided by the legislative assembly.' after the schools/religious/charitable exemption sentence. version_after equals current_text. Verbatim from 2003 CAA.PDF p.2 (450 dpi).
